cases.description: The withdraw execution logic in `executeWithdraw` can be blocked if a receiver is blacklisted, due to missing checks allowing the queue to halt. A user or known blacklisted address can interrupt or block the withdrawal process, potentially locking funds.
cases.vulnerable_code_snippet: // Blacklist check allows halt in queue processing

cases.description: The function `BaseConnector.sendTokensToTrustedAddress` does not sufficiently validate the caller, allowing flash loan contract to move tokens from connectors without authorization. A flash loan caller can withdraw funds from another vault’s connector, enabling theft of assets.

The manager role is granted authority to perform both operational and configuration-level actions without separation of duties. A compromised manager key could reconfigure connectors and manipulate asset flows, increasing blast radius.
cases.vulnerable_code_snippet: // manager role combines operational and config permissions

cases.description: The function removeOwnerAtIndex relies on an index that can change between calls, allowing a different owner to be removed than intended when the owners array is modified. A wallet owner can be unintentionally or maliciously removed, potentially leading to loss of control over the smart wallet.
cases.vulnerable_code_snippet: // owner array index can change between calls

cases.description: The implementation contract uses OpenZeppelin Initializable but does not disable initializers in the constructor, allowing direct initialization of the implementation contract. An attacker can take ownership of the implementation contract and potentially disrupt proxy behavior or upgrades.
Upgrade-related functions rely on ownership that can be improperly configured, allowing unauthorized upgrade paths. A malicious party could upgrade the implementation to arbitrary logic.
The admin role is allowed to modify staking parameters and operational state without time delay or multi-party approval. A compromised admin key could arbitrarily change staking rules, harming users.
cases.vulnerable_code_snippet: // admin role lacks constraint or timelock
